subject: Computer Networking
title: Enterprise Routers
description: A fast breakdown of Enterprise Routers for builders.

hook.text: Enterprise routers don't just connect to one ISP. They connect buildings, campuses, and continents.

buildup.text: They forward based on IP addresses. Routing tables can hold hundreds of thousands of entries.

discovery.text: They run BGP, OSPF, and other protocols to learn the best path to every destination dynamically.

twist.text: Core routers at internet exchange points process terabits per second for millions of users.

climax.text: VRRP lets two routers share a virtual IP. If one fails, the other takes over in milliseconds.

punchline.text: Enterprise routers are the crossroads where networks meet.

quiz.question: What protocol do enterprise routers use to learn internet routes?
